title: Can Jumu'ah prayer be prayed before the sun reaches its zenith (zawal)?
stanceSummary: The **Hanbali madhab** permits praying Jumu'ah **up to an hour before zawal**, based on hadith indicating the Prophet ﷺ sometimes completed Jumu'ah before the sun reached its zenith. The other three schools (Hanafi, Shafi'i, Maliki) hold that Jumu'ah begins only after zawal. Yasir Qadhi holds that following the Hanbali position is **permissible** when a genuine need exists — such as a high school lunch break being the only viable time for students to pray Jumu'ah — in order to preserve the Jumu'ah for those who would otherwise miss it entirely.
alternateQuestions: Can I pray Jumu'ah before the sun starts to decline?
Is it valid to hold Jumu'ah prayer before zawal?
What time does Jumu'ah prayer become permissible?
The Hanbali madhab on Jumu'ah timing
Can a high school hold Jumu'ah during the lunch break before zawal?
transcript:
## Summary of Yasir Qadhi's Position

In a [2021 Ask Shaykh YQ video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GC-Lfw8RfyY) (starting at [0:00](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GC-Lfw8RfyY&t=0s)), Yasir Qadhi addresses the question of whether Jumu'ah can be prayed before the sun reaches its zenith (zawal).

- **Majority position** (Hanafi, Shafi'i, Maliki): Jumu'ah begins only after zawal — the Prophet ﷺ, the Caliphs, and the imams after them prayed after zawal
- **Hanbali madhab**: permits Jumu'ah **up to one hour before zawal**, based on hadith showing the Prophet ﷺ finishing Jumu'ah before the shadows grew long enough for shade, and narrations that the Companions would take their midday siesta and eat lunch *after* Jumu'ah (i.e., Jumu'ah was before midday)
- Yasir Qadhi's position: following the Hanbali madhab is permissible **when there is a genuine need** (e.g., a high school lunch break that ends before zawal is the only opportunity for students to pray Jumu'ah), and the goal is preserving the Deen — not following convenience for its own sake
- **End timing**: three schools say Jumu'ah ends when Asr begins; the Maliki school extends it until shortly before Maghrib

---

## Full Lecture Transcript (Cleaned)

### The Question — [0:00](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GC-Lfw8RfyY&t=0s)

Brother Zahid, a high school student from Wylie, Texas, emails. His school allows Muslim students to pray Jumu'ah during the lunch break on Fridays. However, when daylight saving time changes, the lunch break ends *before* the time of zawal in some months — meaning the only available time slot falls before the start of Zhuhr. He asks: is it permissible to pray Jumu'ah in this time slot?

### A Broader Context — Jumu'ah in America — [2:00](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GC-Lfw8RfyY&t=120s)

Yasir Qadhi first commends the brother for his initiative in establishing Jumu'ah at his high school. He then addresses a broader principle: classical books of fiqh written for Muslim-majority contexts said there should be as few Jumu'ahs as possible in a city. But in Muslim-minority contexts in the West, where workers cannot take 2–3 hours off, where students cannot leave school premises, and where multiple people in large corporations simply cannot reach a masjid during work hours — the priority *must* shift toward facilitating as many Jumu'ahs as possible to help Muslims perform this obligation.

### The Two Positions on Beginning Time — [6:00](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GC-Lfw8RfyY&t=360s)

**Majority position (Hanafi, Shafi'i, Maliki)**: Jumu'ah time begins after zawal — when the sun begins to decline from its zenith. This is supported by narrations that the Prophet ﷺ and the Caliphs always prayed Jumu'ah after zawal.

**Hanbali madhab**: Jumu'ah may be prayed slightly before zawal — specifically within the last hour before zawal (i.e., after the sixth solar hour). Their evidence:

1. The hadith of Jabir: *"We used to pray Jumu'ah with the Prophet ﷺ and then go back to our camels and take them to the pasture as the sun began to decline"* — indicating Jumu'ah was completed before zawal
2. The narration that after Jumu'ah, the Companions would take their midday siesta (*qaylula*) — a practice done around the time of zawal — meaning Jumu'ah ended before that
3. Ibn Qudama, the great Hanbali encyclopedist, states in *al-Mughni* that praying Jumu'ah up to one hour before zawal is fully permissible

### End Time of Jumu'ah — [12:00](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GC-Lfw8RfyY&t=720s)

Three schools (Hanbali, Shafi'i, Hanafi) say Jumu'ah time ends when Asr begins — though they differ on when Asr begins (Hanafis say when the shadow doubles, the others say when it equals the object's length). The Maliki school extends Jumu'ah all the way until before Maghrib as long as Asr is prayed afterward.

### The Ruling for Brother Zahid — [14:00](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GC-Lfw8RfyY&t=840s)

Yasir Qadhi is direct: Brother Zahid and his fellow students **should** follow the Hanbali position and pray Jumu'ah during their lunch break throughout the entire year. The reason is not to make life easier — it is to preserve the Deen of these 15-year-old students, to give them the experience of praying Jumu'ah together, to hear the khutbah, and to feel their Muslim identity on a Friday. That is a legitimate Islamic goal, and choosing the Hanbali position to achieve it is completely valid.

And Allah knows best.
